title: Jerresk

{{Quote|Jerresk has done a masterful job of keeping his base's location a secret. Likewise, he chooses his targets intelligently. This Quarren's activities have resulted in the loss of nine transport ships, in addition to numerous small craft. Fortunately, none of Jerresk's raids have resulted in fatalities, as the crews are packed into escape pods with distress signals. However, I believe that the pirate would have no qualms about killing should it be "necessary" to achieve his aims.|[[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]]|<ref name="WBC" />}}
'''Jerresk''' was a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] who, with [[Nosstrick]], attacked several [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] supply shipments, earning very large [[Bounty/Legends|bounties]] and moving them from the ''[[Hundred Club]]'' to the list of "Most Wanted".

==Biography==
Jerresk was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]] in [[26 BBY/Legends|26 BBY]]. A [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] for renown throughout the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], Jerresk operated a small [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] consisting of a [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Light freighter/Legends|light freighter]], two [[Scout ship/Legends|scout vessels]], and four [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunters]]. The Quarren used complex, but reliable tactics; all his ships had a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] and each was programmed with a pre-calculated [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] escape route in case their attacks went badly. Each ship had a different location to jump to, from which a second hyperspace jump was enacted to the true rendezvous point. Using this methods, Jerresk kept his base's location a secret.<ref name="WBC" />

Jerresk's last known sighting was in the [[Fordon system/Legends|Fordon system]] where he and his pirates assaulted a New Republic supply vessel en route to the [[Musson system/Legends|Musson system]]. The attack was the fourth in a series of raids by Jerresk targeted at the New Republic. The New Republic offered a reward of 8,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for Jerresk's capture on charges of piracy, [[Thief/Legends|theft]] of New Republic property, and assault and battery.<ref name="WBC" />

==Personality and traits==
Jerresk was a [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who was 1.8 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall. He was an able commander, shrewd bargainer and [[Con artist/Legends|con artist]].<ref name="WBC" />

==Skills and abilities==
Jerresk was trained to use [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]], [[blaster artillery]], and vehicle-mounted blasters. He was also skilled in [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee combat]]. Jerresk was knowledgeable about [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]] and languages, and had working knowledge of [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucratic]] procedure and the criminal underworld.<ref name="WBC" />

Jerresk was a [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], able to fly small vessels and [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], as well as operate the weapons and [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]] on small ships. He could also [[Astrogation/Legends|navigate]] through hyperspace, ride beasts, and operate [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] craft. Jerresk was skilled in [[Computer/Legends|computer]] programming and repair, starfighter repair, demolitions, and bypassing security systems.<ref name="WBC" />

==Equipment==
Jerresk was armed with a [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster pistol]].<ref name="WBC" />

==Behind the scenes==
Jerresk was featured in [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']], a [[1993]] sourcebook published by [[West End Games]]. [[Mike Jackson]] provided the illustration for Jerresk.

==Appearances==
